headline: JPMorgan’s 6% AutoZone Stake Highlights Evolving Institutional Ownership Story
summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has disclosed a passive stake of over 6% in AutoZone (NYSE:AZO). The position was reported as of March 31, 2026, in a Schedule 13G filing. This level of institutional ownership represents a meaningful shift in the shareholder base of AutoZone. AutoZone, a major retailer and distributor of automotive replacement parts and accessories, sits at the intersection of consumer spending, vehicle maintenance habits, and the aging car fleet. For investors watching NYSE:AZO, the...

headline: Is It Time To Reconsider AutoZone (AZO) After Recent Share Price Weakness?
summary: If you are wondering whether AutoZone's current share price still offers value after a long run, it helps to first look at how the market has been treating the stock recently. AutoZone closed at US$3,347.28, with the share price down 1.8% over the last week, down 6.3% over the past month, up 1.3% year to date, and down 13.7% over the last year. That mix of shorter term weakness and modest year to date progress, set against a weaker one year result, often reflects shifting views on risk and...
